fields.label: Authentication Provider
fields.description: Specify which AWS credentials chain to use.

fields.label: Credentials Profile Name
fields.description: Credentials profile name, as specified in ~/.aws/credentials, leave blank for default.

fields.label: Assume Role ARN
fields.description:
Optional. Specifying the ARN of a role will ensure that the
                     selected authentication provider is used to assume the role rather than the
                     credentials directly.

validations.message: When set, must be a valid IAM role ARN, e.g. arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/MyRole

fields.label: External ID
fields.description: If you are assuming a role in another account, that has been created with an external ID, specify the external ID here.

fields.label: Endpoint
fields.description: Optionally, specify a custom endpoint for the service

fields.label: Default Region
fields.description: Specify the region, such as for US West (Oregon) use ` us-west-2 ` as the region.

fields.label: Define write permissions for Alarm Configuration Panel
Assume Role ARN Write
fields.description: Specify the ARN of a role to assume when writing property values in IoT TwinMaker

validations.message: When set, must be a valid IAM role ARN, e.g. arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/MyRole

relationships.description: AWS access key pair — supplied together when authType is 'keys'

STS assume-role parameters — external ID is only meaningful when assumeRoleArn is set. TwinMaker's CheckHealth (pkg/plugin/datasource.go:179-184) rejects the datasource when assumeRoleArn is empty, so it is effectively required.

Alarm Configuration Panel write permissions: the switch toggles visibility of the write role ARN input. Toggling the switch off clears assumeRoleArnWriter; a non-empty assumeRoleArnWriter derives the switch back to on at load time.

instructions.msg: Four editor-selectable AWS auth values are stored in jsonData.authType: 'ec2_iam_role' (Workspace IAM Role — no extra credentials needed), 'default' (AWS SDK Default — the default credential chain), 'keys' (Access & secret key — puts accessKey/secretKey in secureJsonData), 'credentials' (Credentials file — optionally provide jsonData.profile). Two more values are storage-valid but not editor-selectable for this plugin: 'grafana_assume_role' (Grafana Assume Role — @grafana/aws-sdk 0.8.3 restricts it to a fixed allow-list of cloudwatch/athena/amazonprometheus, and grafana-iot-twinmaker-datasource is not in that list) and 'arn' (deprecated legacy value the backend maps to 'default').

Minimal payload per auth method. TwinMaker's CheckHealth (pkg/plugin/datasource.go:171-184) requires both jsonData.workspaceId and jsonData.assumeRoleArn to be non-empty regardless of auth type, so every payload must include them. Access & secret key: {"jsonData":{"authType":"keys","defaultRegion":"us-east-1","workspaceId":"<WORKSPACE>","assumeRoleArn":"arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/TwinMakerDashboard"},"secureJsonData":{"accessKey":"<AKID>","secretKey":"<SECRET>"}}. Credentials file: {"jsonData":{"authType":"credentials","profile":"my-profile","defaultRegion":"us-east-1","workspaceId":"<WORKSPACE>","assumeRoleArn":"arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/TwinMakerDashboard"}}. Workspace IAM Role / AWS SDK Default: {"jsonData":{"authType":"ec2_iam_role","defaultRegion":"us-east-1","workspaceId":"<WORKSPACE>","assumeRoleArn":"arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/TwinMakerDashboard"}} (swap authType to 'default').

The assumeRoleArn field is presented as 'Optional' by the shared @grafana/aws-sdk ConnectionConfig, but TwinMaker specifically requires it because IoT TwinMaker calls (GetSessionToken, Scene Viewer, Video Player) need STS-vended credentials scoped by an IoT TwinMaker dashboard role. The editor renders a red Alert (ConfigEditor.tsx:102-114) directing users to https://docs.aws.amazon.com/iot-twinmaker/latest/guide/dashboard-IAM-role.html when assumeRoleArn is empty, and CheckHealth fails outright with 'Assume Role ARN is required' (pkg/plugin/datasource.go:179-184).

Optional write permissions for the AWS IoT TwinMaker Alarm Configuration Panel: jsonData.assumeRoleArnWriter is a second STS role ARN used when the Alarm Configuration Panel writes property values back to IoT TwinMaker (pkg/models/settings.go:63-66 → ToAWSDatasourceSettingsWriter). It is optional — leave it empty (or omit the key) when write access is not needed. The editor gates it behind a 'Define write permissions for Alarm Configuration Panel' switch (ConfigEditor.tsx:141-162): the switch is not persisted, and toggling it off clears assumeRoleArnWriter. On load, a non-empty assumeRoleArnWriter derives the switch state back to on.

secureJsonData values (accessKey, secretKey) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.accessKey == true) instead of secureJsonData. Note: unlike sibling AWS plugins, TwinMaker's backend Load (pkg/models/settings.go:37-38) reads ONLY accessKey and secretKey from decrypted secure data — sessionToken is dropped even though awsds.AWSDatasourceSettings.SessionToken is part of the embedded struct. Setting secureJsonData.sessionToken on a TwinMaker datasource has no effect.

Region handling: jsonData.defaultRegion defaults to 'us-east-1' — the editor sets it on mount if empty (ConfigEditor.tsx:31-36) and the backend Load repeats the fallback (pkg/models/settings.go:30-35). The plugin's supportedRegions list (src/datasource/regions.ts) omits us-east-2, ca-central-1, and every other AWS region not in the inline options; the Select is created with allowCustomValue={true} so unlisted regions can still be typed in. Note: TwinMaker is only available in a subset of AWS regions per AWS's service availability — check https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/iot-twinmaker.html.
